Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Appraiser To Determine Fair Market Value Under Partition Of Real Property Act
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Morgan Sellers, Esq. representing Plaintiff Grace Waikwan Wong.
Hellas Leung, Esq. representing Defendants.

No hearing held.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

AIKWAN WONG TRUST DATED JULY 24, 2024 Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Appraiser To Determine Fair Market Value Under Partition Of Real Property Act

The Court notes that the Appraisal has been on file since May 11 and that Plaintiff's counsel Morgan Sellers took it upon themselves to send the notice required under subdivision (e) on the Court's behalf. No one asked Mr. Sellers to do this. In addition, the Notice contemplates a hearing to determine the Fair Market Value on June 1, 2026. No such hearing was scheduled by the Court. In fact, under subdivision (f), the statute is very clear that a hearing to determine the fair market value cannot take place no sooner than 30 days after a copy of the notice of the appraisal is sent to each party under subdivision (e). Service of the notice is also questionable in that Mr. Sellers electronically served a limited scope attorney and not each party. To cure the deficiencies of the notice and overreach of Plaintiff's counsel, the Court shall send proper notice under subdivision (e), despite it being more than "10 days after the appraisal [was] filed." A hearing is set for July 2, 2026 to determine the fair market value of the property under subdivision (f). Counsel for the moving party is instructed to bring a copy of a Notice that conforms with subdivision (e), which the Court will mail to each party. Counsel for the moving party shall also bring a copy of a proposed order that conforms with this tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's counsel provided the proposed order and notice ordered by the Court.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition, Appointment Referee, And Determination Of Property Value
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Freedman for plaitniff Grace Waikwan Wong.
Hellas Leung for defendants Simon Po Wai Wong and Xueqin Shuai.

The matter was on calendar for Plaintiff's Motion for Interlocutory Judgment of Partition, Appointment of Referee, and Determination of Property Value.

Plaintiff contested the Court's tentative decision to deny the motion. Mr. Freedman argued that the motion properly sought a threshold determination of plaintiff's right to partition and that the Partition of Real Property Act supplements existing law but does not govern the initial determination of that right. He further argued that the right to partition was fully briefed and substantively opposed, and that no procedural objections were raised by defendants.

After hearing oral argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT OF PARTITION, APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE, AND DETERMINATION OF PROPERTY VALUE is DENIED. This action was filed on June 24, 2025 alleging that parties' interest in the property is as tenants in common. (Paragraph 12). Plaintiff appears to seek partition by sale. (See moving papers). Notice of Motion lists no grounds for the motion. Points and Authorities contain limited legal argument and only in passing reference authority. There is no mention of Partition of Real Property Act in the moving papers.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
